MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-01-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS DANIEL HARVEY, AND CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT GRANTS AND DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN FAVOR OF MR. HARVEY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 (BREACH OF CONTRACT) AND CAUSE OF ACTION 4 (GOODS AND SERVICES RENDERED) BECAUSE PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT MR. HARVEY WAS ACTING AS THE EMPLOYEE AND AGENT OF CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD ("C&W"). (UNDISPUTED FACT 1). THE GENERAL RULE IS THAT THE PRINCIPAL (C&W) IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION. SEE CAL. CIVIL CODE SEC. 2337. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON CAUSE OF ACTION 3 (MONEY HAD AND RECEIVED) IN FAVOR OF MR. HARVEY AND C&W AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO MR. HARVEY AND C&W ON CAUSE OF ACTION 2 (INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE BUSINESS ADVANTAGE) BECAUSE DEFENDANTS FAILED TO MAINTAIN THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. DEFENDANTS MERELY RELY ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT AND THE DECLARATION OF MR. HARVEY TO MAINTAIN THAT BURDEN. MR. HARVEY COULD BE LIABLE FOR THIS TORT IF THE TRIER OF FACT DETERMINES THAT HIS CONDUCT WAS WRONGFUL. SEE CAL. CIVIL CODE SEC. 2343. DEFENDANTS' MOTION DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S THEORY OF LIABILITY BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S RELATIONSHIP WITH HEROKU, SALESFORCE'S PURCHASE OF HEROKU, HEROKU'S OCCUPANCY OF 600 TOWNSEND ST. AND HOW DEFENDANTS INDUCED PLAINTIFF TO PART WITH VALUABLE INFORMATION REGARDING THE PROPERTY AND HEROKU WITH THE PROMISE OF A COMMISSION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR # 5963 (302/EHG)
REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JUN-01-2015 - CASE SETTLED WITH COURT SETTLEMENT OFFICER, PANG LY. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 610 ON SEP-15-2015 AT 10:30 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385. (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar May-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER W. CRAIGIE OF THE CRAIGIE, MCCARTHY & CLOW LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 732-7788 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NICHOLAS G. HOOD OF THE WRIGHT FINLAY & ZAK LLP, TEL. NO. (949) 797-1313 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DANIEL HARVEY, AND CUSHMAN & WAKEFIELD OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication Of Issues - The motion for summary judgment is denied. The Court grants and denies the motion for summary adjudication in part. The Court grants summary adjudication in favor of Mr. Harvey as to cause of action 1 (breach of contract) and cause of action 4 (goods and services rendered) because Plaintiff admits that Mr. Harvey was acting as the employee and agent of Cushman & Wakefield ("C&W"). (Undisputed Fact 1). The general rule is that the principal (C&W) is responsible for any contractual obligation. See Cal. Civil Code sec. 2337. The court grants summary adjudication on cause of action 3 (money had and received) in favor of Mr. Harvey and C&W as unopposed. The court denies summary adjudication as to Mr. Harvey and C&W on cause of action 2 (intentional interference with prospective business advantage) because Defendants failed to maintain their initial burden of production. Defendants merely rely on Plaintiff's Complaint and the declaration of Mr. Harvey to maintain that burden. Mr. Harvey could be liable for this tort if the trier of fact determines that his conduct was wrongful. See Cal. Civil Code sec. 2343. Defendants' motion does not sufficiently address Plaintiff's theory of liability based on Plaintiff's relationship with Heroku, Salesforce's purchase of Heroku, Heroku's occupancy of 600 Townsend St. and how defendants induced plaintiff to part with valuable information regarding the property and Heroku with the promise of a commission. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =302/ehg
